channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: John Cena describes how his feelings about death have changed over time
item.description: Each week, a guest draws a card from NPR's Wild Card deck and answers a big question about their life. After 24 years in the WWE ring, John Cena retired from professional wrestling last year.
item.content:encoded: Each week, a guest draws a card from NPR's Wild Card deck and answers a big question about their life. After 24 years in the WWE ring, John Cena retired from professional wrestling last year.

item.title: Venezuelans in Colombia rally to help as deadly earthquakes devastate homeland
item.description: As the death toll from the Venezuelan earthquakes more than doubles, rescue workers continue to search through the rubble for survivors amid worsening conditions and collapsing infrastructure.
item.content:encoded: As the death toll from the Venezuelan earthquakes more than doubles, rescue workers continue to search through the rubble for survivors amid worsening conditions and collapsing infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Manuel Rueda

item.title: Both countries object to the World Cup 'Pride Match' in Seattle
item.description: FIFA designated the June 26th game played in Seattle between Egypt and Iran as a "Pride Match." Both countries objected. How is the queer community in Seattle gearing up for the game?
item.content:encoded: FIFA designated the June 26th game played in Seattle between Egypt and Iran as a "Pride Match." Both countries objected. How is the queer community in Seattle gearing up for the game?
item.dc:creator: Casey Martin

item.title: Tree laws are raising constitutional — and even philosophical — questions
item.description: Many towns have regulations about when a homeowner can cut down their own trees. We examine these laws, and one lawsuit in particular, to learn more about what ownership means in a legal sense.
item.content:encoded: Many towns have regulations about when a homeowner can cut down their own trees. We examine these laws, and one lawsuit in particular, to learn more about what ownership means in a legal sense.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Guo

item.title: Trump's demands for loyalty are causing friction
item.description: President Trump has demanded loyalty from his supporters and allies, causing friction within the Republican party and abroad.
item.content:encoded: President Trump has demanded loyalty from his supporters and allies, causing friction within the Republican party and abroad.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith

item.title: Religious leaders have been talking about aliens for much longer than you think
item.description: God and aliens are having a moment. But what do Christianity and other faiths really teach about ETs?
item.content:encoded: God and aliens are having a moment. But what do Christianity and other faiths really teach about ETs?
item.dc:creator: Jack Jenkins

item.title: What to know about Andy Burnham, the frontrunner for U.K. prime minister
item.description: Born in Liverpool, Burnham went to Cambridge and Westminster. But Manchester made him famous. As mayor, he brought growth to the post-industrial city. Can he scale that nationally as the next U.K. PM?
item.content:encoded: Born in Liverpool, Burnham went to Cambridge and Westminster. But Manchester made him famous. As mayor, he brought growth to the post-industrial city. Can he scale that nationally as the next U.K. PM?
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: A day after Alito's testy response to Sotomayor's dissent, court says it was a 'misunderstanding'
item.description: During Supreme Court opinions Thursday, Justice Samuel Alito, who wrote the majority opinion in an asylum case, appeared to rebut Justice Sonia Sotomayor, who wrote the dissent. The move was highly unusual — and on Friday there was a coda.
item.content:encoded:
During Supreme Court opinions Thursday, Justice Samuel Alito, who wrote the majority opinion in an asylum case, appeared to rebut Justice Sonia Sotomayor, who wrote the dissent. The move was highly unusual — and on Friday there was a coda.

(Image credit: Alex Wong)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: Palisades fire arson case ends in mistrial
item.description: The federal arson case of the man accused of starting the deadly Palisades fire ended in a mistrial on Friday morning.
item.content:encoded: The federal arson case of the man accused of starting the deadly Palisades fire ended in a mistrial on Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Steve Futterman

item.title: Ebon Moss-Bachrach says the ending of FX's 'The Bear' 'feels right to me'
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow talks with Ebon Moss-Bachrach, who plays the brash Richie Jerimovich on FX's The Bear, about what he'll miss most as the show winds down.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow talks with Ebon Moss-Bachrach, who plays the brash Richie Jerimovich on FX's The Bear, about what he'll miss most as the show winds down.
item.dc:creator: Scott Detrow
